1.1: Paulus a Servant of God and an Apostle of Yeshua The Messiah, in the faith of The Chosen Ones of God and the knowledge of the truth which is in the awesomeness of God,
1.2: Concerning the hope of eternal life, which The True God promised before the times of the world.
1.3: And he has revealed his word in his time by our preaching, with which I was entrusted by the commandment of God Our Lifegiver.
1.4: To Titus, a true son in the inner faith: Grace and peace from God The Father and from our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, Our Lifegiver.
1.5: For this reason I had left you in Crete, that you might set right those things that are lacking, and ordain Elders in each city just as I ordered you:
1.6: One who is without fault and has been the husband of one wife, and has believing children who are not abusive and not insubordinate in immorality.
1.7: For an Elder ought to be without faults as a steward of God, and not led by his own mind, neither bad tempered, neither excessive with wine, neither should he be quick to strike with his hand, neither loving filthy riches,
1.8: But he should love strangers, love goodness, should be modest, just, holy, and keep himself from lusts.
1.9: He should care for the teaching of the word of faith, as to be able also to comfort by his sound teaching, and rebuke those who are contentious.
1.10: For there are men who are not submissive and their words are empty; they deceive the minds of people, especially those who are of the circumcision,
1.11: Those whose mouths must be stopped up, for they destroy many households and teach what is not right for the sake of filthy riches.
1.12: One man among them, their own Prophet said, “The children of Crete are always liars, wicked beasts and idle bellies.”
1.13: And he has brought this testimony truthfully; therefore, rebuke them severely that they might be sound in the faith.
1.14: And they should not yield to fables of the Judeans and commands of men who hate the truth.
1.15: For to the pure everything is pure, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving nothing is pure, but their mind and conscience is defiled.
1.16: And they profess that they know God, but by their works they reject him; they are enemies, disobedient and worthless concerning every good work.

2.1: But speak what is fitting for sound teaching.
2.2: And teach that Elders would be watchful in their minds, modest, pure, sound in faith, in love and in patience,
2.3: And the elder women, in this way also, to be fitting in appearance for the worship of God and not to be slanderers, neither subservient to much wine, and to be teachers of excellence.
2.4: And chasten those who are young women to love their husbands and their children,
2.5: And to be chaste and holy, taking good care of their households and obeying their husbands, lest anyone blaspheme the word of God.
2.6: And in this way beseech those who are young men to be serious.
2.7: But show yourself in all things as a model in all good works, and in your teaching let your speech be wholesome,
2.8: Serious, uncorrupted, and let no man despise it, so that he who opposes us may be ashamed, as he will be unable to say anything disgraceful against us.
2.9: Let Servants be subject to their Masters in all things and be pleasing, and let them not resist;
2.10: Neither let them steal, but in all things let them show their good loyalty, to adorn the doctrine of God Our Lifegiver in all things.
2.11: For the all saving grace of God has been revealed to all men;
2.12: And it leads us to reject wickedness and the lusts of the world and to live in this world in purity, in righteousness, and in the worship of God,
2.13: While we look for the blessed hope and the revelation of the glory of The Great God and Our Lifegiver, Yeshua The Messiah,
2.14: Who gave himself in our place to redeem us from all evil and to purify a new people for himself, which is zealous of good works.
2.15: Speak these things; beseech and rebuke with all authority, and no man should despise you.

3.1: Charge them to obey Rulers and Authorities and to submit and be prepared for every good work,
3.2: And not to insult any person, neither to be contending, but they should be humble and show their sweetness in all things to all people.
3.3: For we also from the first were without intelligence and without conviction. We were deceived and Servants to changing lusts and were employed in wickedness and in envy. We were despicable and hating one another.
3.4: But when the sweetness and the loving kindness of God Our Lifegiver was revealed,
3.5: Not by works of righteousness that we had done, but by his own love he gave us life, through the washing of the new birth and the renewing of The Spirit of Holiness,
3.6: Whom he poured upon us richly by Yeshua The Messiah Our Lifegiver,
3.7: That we would be made right by his grace and we would be heirs by the hope in eternal life.
3.8: But the saying is trustworthy, and I want you also to be assuring them in these things, that they who have believed in God would be concerned to cultivate good works, for these things are good and beneficial for the children of men.
3.9: But abstain from foolish debates, from tales of genealogies, from contentions and from the contests of The Scribes, for there is no profit in them and such things are worthless.
3.10: Avoid a man who is a heretic after the first and second admonitions,
3.11: And be aware that he who is such is perverse and a sinner and is self condemned.
3.12: When I have sent to you Artemis or Tukyqos, be diligent to come to me to Niqopolis, for I have set my mind to winter there.
3.13: But concerning Zena the Scribe and concerning Apollo, be diligent to attend well to them, that they would lack nothing,
3.14: And they will teach those who are ours also to cultivate good works in matters of distress, lest they should be fruitless.
3.15: All those who are with me invoke your peace. Invoke the peace of all those who love us in the faith. Grace be with all of you. Amen.
